description: Advanced Package Tool (APT): The core package management system used by Debian, Ubuntu, and related Linux distributions to install, update, configure, and remove software.

commands.explanation: Synchronizes the local package index with the remote repositories. (Run this before upgrades or installations).
Upgrades all installed packages to their newest versions automatically skipping confirmation prompts.
Installs the specified package and its required dependencies.
Removes the specified package but leaves its configuration files intact on the system.
Completely removes the specified package AND deletes all of its associated systemic configuration files.
Automatically removes any packages (such as orphaned dependencies) that were installed by other packages and are no longer needed.
Searches the remote package list for the specified sequence or keyword.
Displays detailed information about a package including its version, size, dependencies, and maintainer.
Adds a new Personal Package Archive (PPA) to your system's software sources list.
Clears out the local repository of retrieved package files (.deb) inside /var/cache/apt/archives/ freeing up disk space.
